description_long: This is a vintage Hitachi television with a boxy, rectangular form and a faux woodgrain finish on the top and sides. The front features a large, convex glass screen bordered by a metallic silver bezel, with two prominent rotary dials for channel and volume control on the right side. Below the dials is a speaker grille and a small area with branding and model information. The back of the television is black plastic, protruding outward to accommodate the cathode ray tube, and includes a panel with various input/output ports. The overall design is robust and utilitarian, typical of late 20th-century analog televisions. The object appears heavy and solid, with a combination of metal, plastic, and wood-patterned materials.
description: A vintage Hitachi television with a woodgrain exterior, large convex glass screen, and silver bezel, featuring rotary dials and a speaker grille on the front.
description_view_0: Front view showing the large glass screen, silver bezel, two rotary dials, speaker grille, and branding.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the woodgrain finish and the depth of the television's back housing.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the black plastic back panel and the input/output port area.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, emphasizing the woodgrain finish and the protruding back.
attribute_bezel_design: Thick silver metallic bezel with a rectangular shape and woodgrain trim, classic vintage styling.
attribute_control_button_layout: Clustered on the front right side, two rotary dials and a button below.
